notes: Primary key. decimal(10,0) carries no fractional digits; INT64 is the most semantically precise BigQuery integer type. Primary key uniqueness must be enforced in BigQuery after every load per acceptance criteria.
NOT NULL at source. BigQuery STRING is unbounded; the varchar(50) length constraint is validated at source during pre-load schema compatibility check.
NOT NULL at source. varchar(50) length constraint validated at source during pre-load check.
NOT NULL at source. varchar(100) length constraint validated at source during pre-load check.
Nullable in source. Null-constraint violations must be logged and reported before the load is marked complete per acceptance criteria.

Partition column — TIME_UNIT partitioning at MONTH granularity. NOT NULL guarantees no rows land in the __NULL__ unpartitioned bucket. Enables efficient partition pruning for date-ranged HR cohort analytics.
Cluster column (position 2). Job role is a frequent GROUP BY and WHERE predicate in HR analytics and reporting queries. varchar(20) constraint validated at source.
NOT NULL. BigQuery NUMERIC (precision 29, scale 9) safely accommodates decimal(8,2) without rounding. Preserves exact monetary precision required for payroll and compensation analytics.
Nullable in source. BigQuery NUMERIC accepts NULL. Exact decimal precision preserved. Null-constraint violations must be logged before load is marked complete per acceptance criteria.
Self-referential FK to employee_id. Nullable — top-level employees have no manager. decimal(10,0) has no fractional digits; INT64 used for consistency with employee_id PK type.

Cluster column (position 1 — highest analytical selectivity). Department is the predominant GROUP BY and WHERE dimension across all HR reporting queries. Nullable in source; decimal(4,0) has no fractional digits warranting INT64.
Cluster column (position 3). Low-cardinality employment status flag enables efficient point-filter pruning within a cluster block. NOT NULL at source.

Record creation timestamp. NOT NULL. Acts as the fallback incremental watermark in the delta extraction query when updated_date IS NULL (COALESCE(updated_date, created_date) >= last_watermark). MySQL TIMESTAMP is UTC; BigQuery TIMESTAMP stores UTC natively — no timezone conversion required.

Primary incremental load watermark column. Nullable — rows with NULL updated_date must fall back to created_date in the delta extraction query. Null-constraint violations must be logged and reported before each incremental load cycle is marked complete per acceptance criteria. MySQL TIMESTAMP is UTC; BigQuery TIMESTAMP stores UTC — no timezone conversion required.
